description: CR delivered in window + live head-owning => suppress

reviews.title: Captured delivered latestRun shape (live ao.db row crosswalk)

latestRun.body:
Changes requested.

I found one blocking reliability regression in the new fail-closed escalation path. The project KB did not have pack-specific Issue 641 notes; the relevant general notes were Guaranteed Delivery and Dead Letter Channel, both pointing to the same requirement here: an undeliverable escalation must still be persisted without the fallback path throwing.

Inline finding:

- scripts/lib/Orchestrator-Escalation.ps1:186: This Write-Error breaks the fail-closed guarantee in the production scripts, because they set $ErrorActionPreference = Stop. In that mode, when both LLM delivery and operator inbox writing fail, this line throws before the function can return { ok = false, healthSpoolPath = ... }; the outer Publish-OrchestratorEscalation catch is interrupted before it persists the fail_closed record. I reproduced it with forced send/inbox failures: the call threw orchestrator escalation operator inbox failed... and the state file was missing. Use non-terminating logging here, or otherwise suppress/contain this error so the fallback result is returned and persisted under ErrorActionPreference=Stop.

I also noticed a non-blocking diagnostic bug in scripts/lib/Review-StartEnvelopeLedger.ps1:254 where $consecutiveFailureCount is never assigned, so the dedupe key and payload omit the actual count. That should be cleaned up, but the inline finding above is the release blocker.

Verification performed: reproduced the failure with $ErrorActionPreference=Stop using forced send and inbox failures; the call threw and no state file was written. git diff --check passed.

Coworker status: installed, but the mandatory large-diff summarization attempt was rejected by the approval policy as external provider data sharing, so I reviewed locally.
